superseded_reason: Incorrectly scored V_8.mov (SURG20 wall-mount recording) while labeling it as V18_video. The true V18.MOV is a separate SURG25 desk-mount recording at 163s/434 FLS. This score's frame observations describe V_8.mov. The V_8.mov file has been re-scored independently as score_claude_V_8_001 (136s/460 FLS), and a corrected V18 score has been written as score_claude_V18_002_corrected (163s/434 FLS). This record is retained as historical artifact only.
recording_notes: Phone filming wall-mounted RCA monitor — different location from V11-V14 lab. 'SURG20 SUPPLIES DO NOT REMOVE' label visible. 'After use, please unplug' instruction on wall. Pink/red foam block. Dark (black) suture. Suture debris visible below trainer from many prior attempts.

frame_analyses.description: At 7 seconds, instrument visible from right near drain. Dark suture extending from drain. Needle loading underway.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Fast entry. Different room/station from V11-V14 lab.

frame_analyses.description: At 30 seconds, both instruments at drain. Curved needle visible above drain being passed through tissue. Suture placement in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement continuing at 30 seconds. Pace consistent with V15/V17 range (30-36s).

frame_analyses.description: At 60 seconds, first throw in progress. Both instruments near drain. Suture X pattern forming. Slit beginning to close.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw underway. Placement completed around 40 seconds.

frame_analyses.description: At 90 seconds, third throw or tightening. Both instruments at drain. Knot visible. Drain narrowing.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw in progress. Throw sequence at good pace.

frame_analyses.description: At 119 seconds, cutting phase. Both instruments crossing at knot area. Suture tails visible. Cutting in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting underway ~20 seconds into cutting phase.

frame_analyses.description: Final frame at 149 seconds. Instruments fully withdrawn. Clean final result: knot visible with dark suture X pattern, short tails. Drain slit appears CLOSED — no visible gap from monitor view. Drain not avulsed, centered on block.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good completion at approximately 145 seconds. Drain closure appears complete — one of the better quality results in the monitor-recorded series.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Monitor view shows no visible gap. Drain slit appears well approximated. Short suture tails. Confidence moderate-low (monitor recording).

technique_summary: V_8 (V18) at 145 seconds is a solid mid-range performance in the V7/V9/V15 tier (142-145s). The drain closure appears complete with no visible gap and short tails — good quality result. Suture placement at 30 seconds is near the V15 level. Throws at 60 seconds total are efficient. Cutting at 45 seconds remains the dominant time sink at 31% of total time. This is a different training station ('SURG20') from the previous lab setups, demonstrating consistent skill transfer across equipment.
improvement_suggestions: Cutting at 45 seconds is the primary bottleneck — 31% of total time
Suture placement at 30s is consistent but 8 seconds slower than peak (22s)
Consider colored suture for better AI scoring visibility
Good quality result — focus on maintaining this closure quality at faster speeds
strengths: No visible gap — good drain closure quality
Efficient throw sequence at 60 seconds total

key_observations: No gap visible — good quality, comparable to V13
Throws 60s — efficient, consistent across all equipment
Different training station (SURG20) — skill transfers well
13s faster than V17, 3s slower than V15 — stable performance band
